title: Rainbow Loom study break
description:
Take a break from studying for finals and do some crafting. We'll have Rainbow Loom materials as well as embroidery floss if you prefer a more classic friendship bracelet. Come when you like, leave when you must.

This program is inspired by the exhibition American Communities 1776-2026, featuring works from Special Collections, on view in the McCabe atrium. Check out the exhibition cases before or after your crafting session.

title: Special Exhibition Tour: American Communities 1776–2026
description:
Join us for a guided tour of American Communities 1776–2026, a special exhibition in McCabe Library marking America250. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today. Meet in the McCabe Library lobby.

This tour complements the Rainbow Loom study break at 2:00-4:00 in McCabe! Take a break from studying for finals and do some crafting. We'll have Rainbow Loom materials as well as embroidery floss if you prefer a more classic friendship bracelet.

title: Workshop: Introduction to Bookbinding (Stab Binding)
description:
Learn stab binding, a technique used in several East Asian cultures, in this hands-on workshop. We'll also have on display a few works from our collections that use this binding. All materials will be provided, and all skill levels are welcome. This workshop is open to all Swarthmore College students, staff, and faculty, but registration is required. Walk-ins will be accommodated on a first-come first-serve basis, as well as if any registrants do not show up by 4:37 PM.

Hosted by the Libraries and the Comics Assistants.

title: Digital Scholarship Research Institute
description: Now in its 8th iteration, the TriCo Digital Scholarship Research Institute is an intensive, cohort-based learning program developed and taught by the TriCo Libraries Digital Scholarship Group. Through instensive workshops, discussions, and project showcases, participants gain experience with fundamental digital scholarship tools and methods but also explore project support and grant opportunities in the Trico and beyond, crafting potential projects and digital assignments, and wayfinding through the maze of possible tools, services, and platforms for digital research. ds-pages.swarthmore.edu…
location: McCabe LibLab, Full Space, Conference Room

title: Worldbuilding Showcase
description:
Join the students of FMST 34: Transmedia Worldbuilding and Storytelling for a presentation of their final projects: four unique imaginary worlds, with screen and material media based on each!

SKOIGLE ISLANDS
With gentle ocean breezes and wide open skies, the Skoigle Islands are a flotilla of giant turtles whose backs have become island terrain. After surviving catastrophic floods caused by humanity’s plundering of the natural environment, humanity has been given a new chance on the Skoigle Islands to rediscover and rekindle its relationship with the new state of nature.

THE EYE
Viridia, an exoplanet filled with dangerous life and psychedelic storms, is currently inhabited by protosapiens (semi-neanderthal/semi-homosapien beings crafted in a lab in the US) living in the planet’s only safe zone: The Eye. After a thousand years of trying to rediscover the remains of the USS Dipper to reconnect with their earthly roots, they finally learn the truth: they are alone.

ARCHIPELAGO OF ASHANTI
The Archipelago of Ashanti on the planet Furth is a formation of islands said to be the remains of two Titans — the supposed corpses of the lovers now host human life, who settled the islands they discovered amidst the sea. However, some instead believe the Ashantai people are the descendants of the Titans, on account of the supernatural abilities displayed by a small fraction of the population. Those who can direct the natural forces of the world through the arts are called mages, and are largely responsible for the advancements which drag Ashanti beyond its otherwise Renaissance-era level of development. However, conflicts between the majority population and minority of magic users have slowly escalated as the latter’s voices and ways are phased out of the government, leaving the Archipelago with a choice: accept the uncontrollable nature of magic, or lose it altogether.

GINKGO’S BILOBA
Ginkgo’s Biloba is a VR game where streamers have been trapped, forced into play to escape by SpectaCorp. The players are accompanied throughout multiple game levels by Ginkgos, virtual companions assigned to each individual upon entry. Each level unlocks a new challenge, taking players to different maps, leading to the ultimate battle royale, determining who will emerge as Spectra’s star streamer.

Presented by the Department of Film and Media Studies
organization: Department of Film and Media Studies

title: Garnet Day
description:
Join our annual tradition of celebrating Swarthmore seniors during May’s Garnet Day! Please note that, since Thursday is the first day of exams, Garnet Day will be on Wednesday this month.

Garnet Day Celebration of the Class of 2026

Wednesday, May 6

4-6 p.m.

Pearson Lawn

There will be carnival games, music, and an array of snacks, including treats from Weckerly’s, Lil’ Pop Shop, and the Dia Dolce cupcake truck. Vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free food options will be available. Everyone’s favorite feathered friend, Phineas, will also make an appearance! 

We hope you can stop by to congratulate our seniors on their remarkable accomplishments, and wish them a wonderful future ahead. As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

description: Join The Swarthmore Chef team for a fun, fast-paced cooking class that teaches the essential skills every beginner needs. In just one hour, participants will learn how to cook perfect eggs, make quick and tasty microwave meals, and build confidence with basic kitchen techniques. The class is upbeat, hands-on, and designed to show that great cooking doesn’t require fancy tools. Students will pick up practical tips they can use immediately in their daily routines.

title: Swat Talk: Laura Hirai `22
description:
In a League of Her Own:
A Conversation with

Women’s Baseball Pioneer Laura Hirai ’22

Please join Laura for a conversation with Ana Apostoleris Rivera ’13 about her pioneering experiences and her insights on the future of women's baseball.

RSVP here

As a young girl, Laura Hirai ’22 fell in love with baseball. Growing up in Japan, she recalls her experience with the sport was not particularly gendered. It was not until she attempted to attend a baseball camp in the US, and was told it was only for boys, that she realized she may face some obstacles to achieving her diamond dreams. Like many Swarthmore students, Laura approached these obstacles as a challenge rather than a deterrent, and at 26 years old has become a global face of women's baseball. Her resume includes being the first, and thus far only, woman to represent Great Britain in U18 baseball; becoming the first "ball girl" for the New York Yankees during the first Major League Baseball game played in England in 2019; and being drafted in the fifth round in the inaugural draft of the US-based Women's Professional Baseball League, which is set to begin play in August. Laura also played varsity softball at Swarthmore.

Laura Hirai ’22 graduated with a Bachelor of Arts in Arabic and Spanish. A dual-sport athlete at the international level, she is also the first person to represent Great Britain in both baseball and softball simultaneously at the national level, pitching at the European Baseball Championships in 2015 and 2017. One of only three European athletes selected in the inaugural Women's Professional Baseball League draft, Laura most recently helped Great Britain claim gold in women's baseball and bronze in softball at the European Championships, with both teams earning World Cup berths.

SwatTalks is an Alumni Council initiative that engages the broader Swarthmore community through webinars featuring alumni and faculty members excelling in their fields. Each hour-long online seminar is free and open to Swarthmore alumni, families, faculty, staff, students, and friends. We welcome suggestions for future speakers and topics — please email your ideas to alumni@swarthmore.edu.

title: Campus Culture & Climate Survey Report Listening Sessions: Staff
description:
Vice President for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Brooke Vick recently shared the results of Swarthmore’s Campus Culture & Climate Survey with our community. The survey captured student, faculty, and staff perspectives on living, learning and working at Swarthmore — you can learn more and find the full report on the Campus Climate Survey website.



What do you think about the survey findings? Do they reflect your experience at the College? What would you like to see come out of this survey, as we plan together for the future of Swarthmore?

Meals for ALL in-person sessions are covered by the Office of DEI.

If you can’t make these sessions, you can attend another one of the sessions listed on the Events page of the Campus Climate Survey website (please log in with your Swarthmore credentials to view), share your input through our feedback form, or email culturesurvey@swarthmore.edu.

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: SwatPDX Book Club
description:
We’re on our 4th SwatPDX book club meeting and would love to have you join us on Tuesday, May 12, 7-9 p.m. The book selection is Train Dreams by Denis Johnson. The meeting address is an alum’s Portland home in Raleigh Hills and will be sent to attendees a few days before the meeting.
 
Please RSVP for the book club by May 5. The form includes a section to indicate your interest in future book club meetings and to receive communications about other SwatPDX events, even if you can’t attend the May book club meeting.

We hope to see you at one of our events!

Sincerely,

Ana Cragnolino ’93, Catherine Rose ’97, and Becky Voorheis ’93
SwatPDX co-leads

title: Peter van de Kamp Observatory Open House
description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: Philadelphia Connection Woodmere Art Museum Tour
description:
Woodmere Art Museum Tour

Charles Knox Smith Hall
9201 Germantown Avenue
Philadelphia

$15 per person
RSVP Today

This landmark exhibition celebrates the fifty-year career of former Swarthmore professor Syd Carpenter. Together, we will explore her evocative ceramic sculptures that weave together African American history, ancestral lands, and the beauty of the natural world. It is a wonderful opportunity to engage with profound art while reconnecting with your local alumni community. Then join us for an optional group lunch after the tour to continue our conversations. Lunch venue details will be shared very soon.

Warmly,
The Philadelphia Connection Committee

title: Alumni Weekend 2026
description:
Whether you are celebrating a milestone reunion (class years ending in 1 and 6) or simply looking for a reason to come back to campus, alumni of all ages are invited to come home to Swarthmore. 


The Class of 1976 will be invited to campus a day early — Thursday, May 28th — in honor of their 50th Reunion!

title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Staff Summer Reading: True Crime Meet-Up
description: Love true crime? Find fellow readers of the genre, share recommendations, and borrow library books that speak to your interests! This program is part of Staff Summer Reading, brought to you by Swarthmore College Libraries, PSAC, the Office of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion, and a Be Well mini grant. Light refreshments will be served.

title: Group Circuit Training with Chris
description: Do you struggle to figure out which workout to do at the gym, or do you need some extra motivation? If so, this class is for you. Receive personal attention to help you improve your overall fitness through a total body workout. The class will offer modifications or challenges to suit each participant. Missed a workout? Review past workouts online.
location: Matchbox Lower Level Lewis Reynolds Performance Center

title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 21**
location: Matchbox Wellness Lounge Upper Level, Matchbox Tarble Commons Upper Level

title: Garnet Day
description:
Join our annual tradition of celebrating Swarthmore seniors during May’s Garnet Day! Please note that, since Thursday is the first day of exams, Garnet Day will be on Wednesday this month.

Garnet Day Celebration of the Class of 2026

Wednesday, May 6

4-6 p.m.

Pearson Lawn

There will be carnival games, music, and an array of snacks, including treats from Weckerly’s, Lil’ Pop Shop, and the Dia Dolce cupcake truck. Vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free food options will be available. Everyone’s favorite feathered friend, Phineas, will also make an appearance! 

We hope you can stop by to congratulate our seniors on their remarkable accomplishments, and wish them a wonderful future ahead. As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

title: Peter van de Kamp Observatory Open House
description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room
